title: eriogonum ovalifolium var. vineum
intro: Eriogonum ovalifolium var. vineum, also known as Vineleaf Buckwheat or Vineleaf Desert Buckwheat, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Eriogonum ovalifolium var. vineum is typically used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in salves and creams for topical application.
